classifications.phrase: Inflation has fallen substantially since the peak in 2022
annual trimmed mean inflation was below 3 per cent for the first time since 2021
headline inflation, at 2.4 per cent, remained within the target band
underlying inflation is now expected to be around the midpoint of the 2–3 per cent…
weaker outlook for growth, employment and inflation in Australia
weaker outlook for growth, employment and inflation in Australia
growth in unit labour costs remains high
risks to inflation have become more balanced
upside risks appear to have diminished
an easing in monetary policy at this meeting was appropriate
this move will make monetary policy somewhat less restrictive

payload.rationale: The RBA signals a dovish stance by emphasizing falling inflation, balanced risks, and the appropriateness of easing policy, while acknowledging persistent labour market tightness and high unit labour cost growth as offsetting hawkish considerations.
